title: Introducing Attention: Direct Paths
concept: Attention creates direct paths between the decoder and all encoder states, letting the decoder focus on relevant words on demand.

exercises.prompt: Why do traditional encoder-decoder models struggle with very long sentences?

options.text: They forget details from the beginning of the sentence.
They translate the sentence into numbers instead of words.
They stop translating entirely and freeze.

exercises.explain: When sentences get too long, a single summary vector cannot hold all the details, causing the model to forget the beginning of the sentence.

cards.title: The Memory Shortcut
cards.body: Imagine trying to translate a whole sentence, but you are only allowed to remember a single, tiny summary word. Hard, right? That's the bottleneck. Let's see how Attention gives us a direct shortcut to the source!

cards.title: What is Attention?
cards.body: Attention is a mechanism in translation models that creates direct, open paths between the generator (decoder) and every single step of the reader (encoder). This lets the model focus on any word at any time.

cards.title: The Open Book
cards.body: Imagine translating a story book by book. Traditional models only read the summary on the back cover. Attention is like having the full book open in front of you, letting you flip back directly to any page you need, whenever you need it!

cards.title: Direct Connections in Action
cards.body: When translating 'The black cat', the decoder needs to write 'chat' (cat). Instead of guessing from a compressed summary, Attention points a direct arrow back to the encoder's state for 'cat', retrieving its exact meaning instantly.

cards.title: Focus, Not Flood
cards.body: Some think Attention forces the model to look at every word equally all the time. Actually, Attention lets the model dynamically decide which words are important and ignore the rest!

cards.title: Bottleneck vs. Direct Paths
cards.body: See how the connection structure changes when we add Attention.

right.text: Every step has its own direct line to every single input word.

left.text: All information is squeezed through one single state at the very end.

exercises.prompt: In traditional translation models, what is the 'information bottleneck'?

options.text: A tiny, single-core processor inside the computer
Forcing all information through a single, small summary state
The model running out of training data
Translating words too quickly without thinking

exercises.explain: Without attention, the decoder only gets a single, highly compressed summary vector from the end of the encoder, which acts like a bottleneck.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Attention allows the decoder to look back at any state of the encoder.
exercises.explain: Attention gives the decoder access to all encoder states, allowing it to look back at any part of the input sentence.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the description of Attention.
exercises.sentence: Attention creates a ___ path from the decoder to every encoder state.
exercises.explain: Attention creates direct paths to all encoder states so the model doesn't have to rely on a single compressed summary.

exercises.prompt: Which of these describes a system using an Attention mechanism?

options.text: A system that reads an entire document and translates it word-for-word without ever looking back.
A model translating a sentence that dynamically highlights and refers back to the exact source words it needs for each step.
A human reading a list of words aloud while paying equal attention to every letter.

exercises.explain: AI models use Attention to selectively focus on specific parts of the input sequence. Humans reading everything with equal weight is not how Attention behaves.

exercises.prompt: How does the Attention mechanism solve the 'information bottleneck' problem in translation?

options.text: By making the bottleneck smaller so the model works faster
By forcing the decoder to only look at the very last word
By building direct paths to all encoder states, bypassing the bottleneck
By erasing the encoder completely to save memory

exercises.explain: Attention solves the information bottleneck by creating a direct pathway between the decoder and every single encoder state, allowing it to look at any part of the source sentence at any time.
